ddls:
CREATE TABLE "interrupt_events" (
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interrupt event */,
  "interrupt_name" TEXT /* Name of the interrupt event (e.g., Alarm A, Wakeup) */,
  "interrupt_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt type associated with the interrupt event */,
  "tamper_detection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tamper detection mechanism associated with the interrupt event */,
  "event_flag" TEXT /* Event flag associated with the interrupt event */,
  "enable_bit" TEXT /* Enable bit for the interrupt event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the interrupt event */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the interrupt event was created */,
  "modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the interrupt event was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interrupt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_events_interrupt_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("interrupt_type_id") REFERENCES interrupt_event_types ("interrupt_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_events_tamper_detection_id FOREIGN KEY ("tamper_detection_id") REFERENCES tamper_detection ("tamper_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interrupt configuration */,
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt event being configured */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the interrupt event */,
  "interrupt_source" TEXT /* Source of the interrupt event (e.g., alarm, wake-up, tamper detection) */,
  "enable_status" TEXT /* Enable status of the interrupt configuration */,
  "exit_sleep_mode" TEXT /* Exit mode for sleep mode (yes/no) */,
  "exit_stop_mode" TEXT /* Exit mode for stop mode (yes/no) */,
  "exit_standby_mode" TEXT /* Exit mode for standby mode (yes/no) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the interrupt configuration was created */,
  "modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the interrupt configuration was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_configurations_interrupt_source FOREIGN KEY ("interrupt_source") REFERENCES interrupt_sources ("interrupt_source_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_configurations_priority FOREIGN KEY ("priority") REFERENCES interrupt_priorities ("priority_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_configurations_interrupt_id FOREIGN KEY ("interrupt_id") REFERENCES interrupt_events ("interrupt_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_priorities" (
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each priority level */,
  "priority_level" INTEGER /* Priority level (1-10) */,
  "priority_name" TEXT /* Name of the priority level (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("priority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exit_modes" (
  "exit_mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exit mode */,
  "exit_mode_name" TEXT /* Name of the exit mode (e.g., sleep, stop, standby) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the exit mode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("exit_mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role associated with the user */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team associated with the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt configuration being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the configuration */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method of access (e.g., web interface, API) */,
  "access_status" TEXT /* Status of the access event (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES interrupt_configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt configuration being versioned */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., active, archived) */,
  "version_comment" TEXT /* Comments or notes about the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES interrupt_configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_event_types" (
  "interrupt_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interrupt event type */,
  "interrupt_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the interrupt event type (e.g., alarm, wake-up) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the interrupt event type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interrupt_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tamper_detection" (
  "tamper_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tamper detection */,
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt event associated with tamper detection */,
  "tamper_type" TEXT /* Type of tamper detection */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the tamper detection mechanism */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tamper_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., engineer, developer, QA) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_sources" (
  "interrupt_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interrupt source */,
  "interrupt_source_name" TEXT /* Name of the interrupt source (e.g., alarm, wake-up, tamper detection) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the interrupt source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interrupt_source_id")
)
